label: Uncomplicated heat rash without heat illness or infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated heat rash.
No heat illness, dehydration, secondary infection, severe/widespread rash, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Heat exhaustion, heat stroke, syncope, confusion, dehydration, persistent vomiting, or unstable vital signs.
Cellulitis, abscess, pus, fever, severe pain, blistering/peeling rash, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, or systemic illness.
Pregnancy, pediatric pathway, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or urgent care if symptoms are not improving or if the rash keeps coming back.

source_audit.notes: CDC supports heat-rash framing and cooling/drying measures while MedlinePlus supports heat-illness red flags. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated heat rash without heat illness, dehydration, secondary infection, severe/widespread or blistering/peeling rash, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. CDC supports heat-rash framing and cooling/drying measures while MedlinePlus supports heat-illness red flags. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated heat rash without heat illness, dehydration, secondary infection, severe/widespread or blistering/peeling rash, systemic illness, pregnancy/pediatric/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated heat rash
no heat illness, dehydration, cellulitis, secondary infection, severe/widespread rash, or systemic illness documented
no high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
